_note: THE RELIC MARKET — a pure-data probe of N-WAY CONTESTED SIMULTANEITY, the last flagged edge. Every ~6 ticks the auction house produces ONE relic; six traders all want it, same tick, same claim. HYPOTHESIS: sequential in-tick resolution means the LOWEST-ID trader wins every single contest (deterministic priority = a fairness pathology for 'simultaneous' contention). TEST A (this file, eager=1): everyone grabs every tick — measure the winner spread in the chronicle. TEST B (set stagger to chance-gated): each trader only reaches for it some ticks — does the spread de-bias? If yes, fairness under contention is a DATA pattern, not a missing prim.

generators._sentinel_note: child_* aggregators have NO kind filter (same asymmetry as here_*) — the fairness events below want min/max over TRADERS only, so every non-trader carries a sentinel trophies=999 to stay out of child_min's way. Composes, but it is the third world to hit kind-filterless aggregation.

actions.name: go_to_the_highest_bidder
actions._note: TEST C — the pick-link prim: the RELIC selects its owner, the argmax of a fresh per-tick bid (keen*rand). Contention resolves by MERIT+DICE, not entity id. This inversion (the contested thing chooses) is the fair-auction pattern.

actions.name: bank_the_relic
actions._note: release the prize so contention REPEATS: mark it banked (it despawns itself), drop the edge, count the trophy. claim is 1:1 both ways — a holder can't claim again until they release.

events.label: 🔔 the auctioneer unveils a fresh relic
the relic disappears into a private vault
🏆 snatches the relic from six reaching hands
⚖ every trader has won at least once — a fair floor
⚠ 30 relics gone and someone has NOTHING — an unfair floor
